title: Cooling-Mode Rank of a Thermal-Coupling Tensor
strategies: schoolbook CP decomposition: one rank-1 cooling mode per non-zero coupling coefficient (rank = nnz(T) = the checker baseline)
fixed-axis slicing: decompose each zone slice (I x J matrix) by exact column-row rank factorisation; rank = K * min(I,J)
best-axis slicing: try all three slicing directions and keep the cheapest; rank = product of the two smallest dimensions

genuine low-rank search (unknown optimum): the tensor is overcomplete-rank so the planted factors are not polynomially recoverable (Jennrich fails); any approach beating best-axis slicing wins head-room
